codeReview.summary: The feature partially implements the intended behavior (vLLM capacity-gate bypass and 404 status checks), but two blocking requirements are not satisfied: model-info still omits `props` instead of returning `null`, and vLLM start still executes a Docker command before compose generation failure paths.

issues.description: VAL-API-003 / expectedBehavior requires `/api/model-info` to return `props: null` for vLLM `/props` 404s, but `ModelInfoResponse.props` is annotated with `#[serde(skip_serializing_if = "Option::is_none")]`, so `None` is omitted instead of serialized as JSON null. The added test also codifies omission (`props should be omitted when None`, line 2016), which conflicts with the feature contract.

VAL-CROSS-002 requires compose-generation config errors to return before any Docker commands execute. `VllmBackend::start()` calls `Self::check_docker_available().await?` before `compose::generate_compose(...)`; `check_docker_available` runs `docker compose version` (line 243), so Docker is invoked even on invalid-config compose-generation failure paths.

summary: Review failed with 2 blocking issues: `/api/model-info` still omits `props` instead of returning `null`, and vLLM start still invokes Docker before compose-generation failures are surfaced.
